title: Implement strict commit gating and repo-state enforcement
summary: Implement the Phase 2 commit workflow changes for v0.5.0 so commit operations reflect the rules-defined work loop more explicitly and safely. The work should require successful verification before commit, make repository-state awareness part of commit decisioning, and prevent commit attempts when there are no changes to record, turning current helper behavior into a clearer protocol-level guarantee.
outputs: Commit workflow that explicitly requires verified transaction state before commit start
Repository-state-aware commit gating that checks whether changes exist before attempting git commit
Clear failure behavior and diagnostics when commit is attempted without verification or without pending changes
Regression coverage for verified-state and repository-state commit preconditions
acceptance_criteria: Commit start is rejected when the active transaction has not reached verified state through the canonical verify flow.
Commit workflow checks repository change state before attempting a commit and refuses to proceed when there is nothing to commit.
The no-changes case is treated as an explicit workflow precondition failure rather than relying on a downstream git failure message.
Commit helpers expose enough observable context to show that verified state and repository-state gating were evaluated before commit start.
Commit behavior remains aligned with canonical event ordering, including verify pass before commit start and commit result after commit start.
Regression tests cover commit rejection for unverified state and commit rejection when the repository has no changes.
tasks: Review the current commit helper and repository-state tooling paths to identify where commit preconditions are implicit rather than explicitly enforced.
Implement explicit gating that requires the active transaction to be in verified state before commit can begin.
Integrate repository status inspection into commit decisioning so the workflow can determine whether changes exist before invoking git commit.
Return direct workflow-level failures for no-changes and not-verified cases instead of depending on lower-level git errors.
Ensure commit-related diagnostics and summaries make the precondition evaluation visible enough for client and operator understanding.
Add or extend regression tests that assert commit rejection for unverified transactions and for repositories with no pending changes.
notes: This ticket corresponds to the 0.5.0 draft gap that identifies commit workflow enforcement as weaker than the rules describe.
Keep the implementation strict: the goal is to elevate commit policy from possible helper behavior to an enforced protocol property.
Prefer explicit precondition checks with actionable diagnostics over allowing commit attempts to fail later at the git layer.
Ensure any repository-state snapshot used for commit gating is consistent with the transaction context seen by the commit helper.
